text.title: Mapping recoveryHint to loop policy
text.summary: Each recoveryHint enum value corresponds to a specific action the agent loop must take — make this mapping explicit in the runner.

section.title: Hint → action table
section.p: The whole point of a closed enum is deterministic dispatch. The agent runner inspects `recoveryHint` and routes the next step without involving the model again, saving a turn and avoiding hallucinated retry policies.

rule.statement: The agent runner MUST map each `recoveryHint` value to exactly one action: `RETRY_LATER` → wait `retry_after_seconds` (clamped 1-300s) and retry the same call up to a per-tool retry budget; `CHECK_INPUT` → return the error to the model with no automatic retry; `TRY_ALTERNATIVE` → return to the model, signal to pick a different tool; `REPORT_TO_USER` → halt the loop, surface to user; `NEEDS_AUTH` → halt the loop, request credentials.
rule.rationale: Without an explicit mapping, the model invents its own retry policy each turn, often retrying `INVALID_ARGUMENT` (waste) or escalating `RATE_LIMITED` (premature). Explicit dispatch is cheaper and safer.

list.item: `RETRY_LATER` — transient (rate limit, 503, network blip). Runner sleeps and retries automatically.
`CHECK_INPUT` — argument problem (validation, bad ID). Send back to model so it can rethink.
`TRY_ALTERNATIVE` — this tool cannot serve the request (wrong scope, missing permission). Model picks a different tool.
`REPORT_TO_USER` — irrecoverable for the agent (data inconsistency, business rule violation). Halt, surface.
`NEEDS_AUTH` — credentials missing or expired. Halt, request via the user-facing flow.

example.note: Closed enum + closed table. Every hint has exactly one outcome. Retry budget bounds the loop.

antipattern.description: Returning the error envelope to the model on every hint and letting the model decide whether to retry.
antipattern.reason: Burns tokens, invites hallucinated retry decisions, and bypasses the runner's retry budget — the loop can spin forever.

section.reference: Arun Baby — Error handling and recovery in AI agents.
